title: Agent workers
description: Configure you-agent-factory AGENT_WORKER backends for AGENT_RUN loops, including model identity, agentTools.policy, and agent-run failure cautions.
openingSummary: Agent workers supply the model backend and shared system-prompt body for prompt-rendered AGENT_RUN workstations. Use this page when you need the durable web contract for AGENT_WORKER authoring, tool policy, and agent-run failure recovery.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: An Agent worker is the model-backed executor for prompt-rendered AGENT_RUN workstations in you-agent-factory. This reference covers AGENT_WORKER identity, how one agent loop runs per dispatch, where model and modelProvider belong, explicit agentTools.policy, bounded tool diagnostics, and recognizable agent-run failure classes. Use it when you need to author an agent backend without confusing it with one-shot inference operations.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: An AGENT_WORKER supplies the model backend and the markdown body used as the shared system prompt for every AGENT_RUN workstation that binds that worker name. The factory runtime owns one agent loop per dispatch, including session cancellation, timeout, and final-result selection. Customers configure agent behavior through factory vocabulary—AGENT_WORKER, AGENT_RUN, and worker or workstation fields—rather than by invoking an external harness CLI. Agent workers are not INFERENCE_WORKER backends: they do not declare operations, and validation rejects capability declarations on AGENT_WORKER.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author an AGENT_WORKER under workers/<name>/AGENTS.md with type AGENT_WORKER. Put model and modelProvider on the worker when you need authored identity; authored values always win over operator defaults. Keep the shared system prompt in the markdown body. Bind the worker from an AGENT_RUN workstation. Runner selection is separate from modelProvider—choose the built-in runner through factory or workstation runner fields, not by overloading modelProvider.

toolPolicy.title: Explicit Tool Policy
toolPolicy.body: Agent tool use is disabled unless the worker declares an explicit agentTools.policy. Omit agentTools to keep the default DISABLED behavior. agentTools is valid only on AGENT_WORKER; validation fails when tool configuration is present without a policy, when the policy is unsupported, or when agentTools appears on a non-agent worker type.

failureAndSecurity.title: Failure And Security Cautions
failureAndSecurity.body: Agent dispatches surface stable failure_class values in work and session inspection metadata. Use the recovery hints below for lease and model readiness, tool-policy violations, harness failures, and timeout or cancel paths. Do not treat INFERENCE_WORKER or operations as an agent-loop substitute—validation rejects capability declarations on AGENT_WORKER. Keep secrets out of worker bodies; allowed tool execution records only bounded tool_diagnostics summaries.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web Agent workers reference for AGENT_WORKER / AGENT_RUN authoring, tool policy, and agent-run failure recovery. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not the full Workers taxonomy overview, not workstation routing or lifecycle depth, not resource-capacity deep-dive, and not the Inference workers full contract. Sibling pages aid discovery; they are not required to define what an Agent worker is.

links.ownershipIntro: Put backend identity on the worker. Authored model and modelProvider values win over operator defaults from config, environment, or global flags.
links.ownershipWorkerFields: type, model, modelProvider, executorProvider, openCodeAgent, agentTools, shared system-prompt body
links.ownershipWorkstationFields: type AGENT_RUN, worker binding, prompt body, limits, outputs, onContinue, onRejection, onFailure
links.ownershipRunnerNote: Runner selection is separate from modelProvider. Use factory or workstation runner fields for built-in runner IDs such as codex, gemini, kiro, cursor-cli, or opencode.
links.minimalExampleLabel: Minimal AGENT_WORKER with authored model identity:
links.minimalExample:
---
type: AGENT_WORKER
model: gpt-5-codex
modelProvider: CODEX
---

You are a software engineer. Follow the workstation instructions and keep changes scoped to the current work item.
links.policyDisabledBehavior: Default. The agent loop runs with tool execution disabled. Tool calls are denied with stable diagnostics instead of being silently ignored.
links.policyReadOnlyBehavior: Allows bounded filesystem inspection tools: read_file and list_directory under the dispatch working directory. Write tools are denied.
links.policyEnabledBehavior: Allows read_file, list_directory, and write_file under the dispatch working directory. Paths outside the working directory are rejected.
links.toolPolicyExample:
---
type: AGENT_WORKER
model: gpt-5-codex
modelProvider: CODEX
agentTools:
  policy: READ_ONLY
---

You are a software engineer. Use read-only filesystem tools only when the workstation prompt requires repository inspection.
links.toolDiagnosticsNote: Allowed tool execution records bounded tool_diagnostics summaries for tool start, success, and failure. Diagnostics do not expose raw process output, secrets, or unrestricted host paths as primary customer results.
links.failureLeaseRecovery: Retry later or increase MODEL resource capacity
links.failureModelReadyCause: Managed runtime missing or still loading
links.failureModelReadyRecovery: Pull or wait for readiness before retrying
links.failureToolPolicyRecovery: Change tool policy or workstation prompt expectations
links.failureHarnessRecovery: Inspect dispatch failure details and factory logs
links.failureTimeoutRecovery: Increase limits or narrow workstation scope
links.failureCanceledRecovery: Resubmit or resume according to workflow policy
links.inferenceSubstituteCaution: Do not treat INFERENCE_WORKER or operations as a substitute for an agent loop. Inference workers declare one-shot model operations for INFERENCE_RUN. Agent workers do not declare operations; validation rejects capability declarations on AGENT_WORKER.
links.siblingDiscoveryIntro: Continue with these sibling surfaces when you need the Workers taxonomy, workstation routing, resource capacity, model-inference tokens, tool calling, logs, troubleshooting, or the Inference workers contract. Sibling links aid discovery; they are not required to define what an Agent worker is.
